facts.zh: “人工智能”一词诞生于 1956 年的达特茅斯会议，被视为 AI 作为独立学科的起点。
facts.en: The term "artificial intelligence" was coined at the 1956 Dartmouth workshop, often seen as the birth of AI as a field.

facts.zh: Transformer 架构来自 2017 年的论文《Attention Is All You Need》，几乎是当今所有大模型的基础。
facts.en: The Transformer architecture comes from the 2017 paper "Attention Is All You Need", the basis of nearly all modern large models.

facts.zh: “神经网络”最早的灵感来自对生物神经元的简化建模，可追溯到 1943 年的 McCulloch–Pitts 模型。
facts.en: Neural networks were first inspired by simplified models of biological neurons, dating back to the 1943 McCulloch–Pitts model.

facts.zh: GPU 之所以适合训练 AI，是因为深度学习的核心运算是大规模并行的矩阵乘法。
facts.en: GPUs suit AI training because the core operation of deep learning is massively parallel matrix multiplication.

facts.zh: “图灵测试”由阿兰·图灵在 1950 年提出，用对话能否被识破来衡量机器智能。
facts.en: The "Turing Test", proposed by Alan Turing in 1950, gauges machine intelligence by whether its conversation can be told apart from a human's.

facts.zh: 模型的“参数量”大致对应它能记住与表达的知识容量，但并不直接等于“更聪明”。
facts.en: A model's parameter count roughly reflects its capacity to store and express knowledge, but more parameters don't always mean smarter.

facts.zh: “幻觉”指模型自信地生成看似合理但实际错误的内容，是当前大模型的主要风险之一。
facts.en: "Hallucination" is when a model confidently generates plausible-sounding but incorrect content, a major risk in today's large models.

facts.zh: AlphaGo 在 2016 年击败李世石，是强化学习与深度学习结合的里程碑事件。
facts.en: AlphaGo's 2016 victory over Lee Sedol was a milestone for combining reinforcement learning with deep learning.
